theme: surveyor placing mile markers on a mountain road
title: Benchmark Stakes on the Mountain Road
seed_problem: 1-D k-median / facility location under a weighted query distribution
mutation: composite: symmetric k-median mutated into a ONE-SIDED (asymmetric) facility-location cost derived from a directional walk-and-probe reading process (a stake only serves queries at or beyond it), coupled with a discrete W-quantized probe-count metric (space-probe-duality), applied against a query trace whose weight mass is organized into multiple hotspot clusters of varying width/weight/location (query-hotspot-mining).
strategies: trivial: ignore the trace, space K stakes uniformly across the road (reproduces the checker's uniform-spacing baseline)

naive greedy: try several standard SYMMETRIC 1-D k-median/k-means recipes (quantile/equal-length bucketing, Lloyd's algorithm from uniform or farthest-point seeds, greedy incremental facility placement) each of which plants stakes at a bucket's WEIGHTED MEDIAN or MEAN -- the textbook-correct facility choice for symmetric L1/L2 distance, wrong here -- and keeps whichever recipe scores best; still never reasons about which side of a stake a query falls on

insight (strong): recognize the distance is one-sided (a stake only helps positions at or beyond it), so within any group the best stake sits at the group's LEFT EDGE, not its median; combine this with a DP over an optimal weighted partition of the trace (not a fixed bucket cut) to decide which positions share a stake, using the W-quantized probe cost directly in the DP
